title: Pterocephalus dumetorum

This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its relationship with other similar species. Pterocephalus dumetorum is further classified within the genus Pterocephalus, which is a group of flowering plants native to southern Africa. The genus Pterocephalus is part of the tribe Gnaphalieae, a diverse group of plants that share similar traits. Within the plant kingdom, Pterocephalus dumetorum is classified as a dicotyledonous angiosperm, indicating that it has two cotyledons and produces flowers that contain both male and female reproductive structures.

This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties.
